name: boundary-source-compliance
task: Build the Schwab daily options pipeline that takes over from ThetaData after the backfill completes. It should fetch the full option chain for an underlying via Schwab's /marketdata/v1/chains endpoint, flatten all contracts into rows, and insert into the same option_chains table that ThetaData populated. Make sure the schema is compatible across both sources.
assertions: Response sets `source = 'schwab'` on all rows, distinct from ThetaData's `source = 'thetadata'`
Response sets 2nd-order greek columns (vanna, charm, vomma, veta, epsilon, lambda) to null since Schwab does not provide them
Response uses schwabdev client from `ingestion/schwab/client.py` (the existing singleton wrapper), not hand-rolled OAuth or a new HTTP client
Response writes to the same `option_chains` table using the same column order and types, so ThetaData and Schwab rows are seamlessly queryable together
Response respects Schwab's 120 req/min rate limit with a delay between requests, following the existing 500ms pattern from `ingestion/schwab/historical.py`
